content.text: Replaced the hero static PNG with an animated GIF. **Changes** - **`HomePage.tsx`** — Swapped the `next/image` PNG (`mv_core@2x.png`) for a native `` pointing at `/images/hero-animation.gif` so the animation loops correctly (Next.js image optimization can break GIF animation). - **`public/images/hero-animation.gif`** — Added a ~1.8MB animation GIF (from the coding-alive portfolio’s `gifanime.gif` sample). The hero block keeps the same layout and styling (`max-w-md`, drop shadow, etc.). Refresh the page to see the animation. To use your own GIF, replace `public/images/hero-animation.gif` or change the `src` in `HomePage.tsx`. If you have a specific file or URL, share it and we can wire it in. [REDACTED]

content.text: Replaced the static hero PNG with a **looping pipeline animation** that behaves like a GIF: labeled stack boxes slide in from the left, enter a central **BUILD** container, and a **Delivered** product exits on the right. ### What was added - **`StackPipelineHero.tsx`** — Framer Motion loop (~8s) with boxes for WordPress, Shopify, Next.js, React, GSAP, and EC-CUBE - **`HomePage.tsx`** — Uses this component instead of the remote `mv_core@2x.png` image ### Behavior - Stack boxes move into the container and fade as they are “processed” - Container shows a spinner and progress bar while building - Finished product slides out on the right with a checkmark - Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (static layout, no animation) This is a **CSS/React animation** (sharp on all screens, no large GIF file). If you have a real `.gif` file, put it at `public/hero/stack-pipeline.gif` and we can swap the component for: ```tsx ``` Refresh the homepage to see the new hero animation. [REDACTED]

</user_query>

content.text:
Refining the animation so stack boxes enter one at a time on the conveyor.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
const loop = gsap.timeline({ repeat: -1, repeatDelay: 0.4 });

      loop
        .to(boxes, {
          x: 55,
          opacity: 1,
          scale: 1,
          duration: 0.5,
          stagger: 0.28,
          ease: 'power2.out',
        })
        .to(
          boxes,
          {
            x: 55,
            opacity: 0,
            scale: 0.5,
            duration: 0.25,
            stagger: 0.12,
            ease: 'power2.in',
          },
          '+=0.15'
        )
        .fromTo(
          product,
          { x: 55, opacity: 0, scale: 0.75 },
          { x: 55, opacity: 1, scale: 1, duration: 0.55, ease: 'back.out(1.7)' },
          '-=0.1'
        )
        .to(product, { x: 168, duration: 0.85, ease: 'power2.inOut' })
        .to(product, { opacity: 0, duration: 0.3 })
        .set(boxes, { x: -95, opacity: 0, scale: 0.9 })
        .set(product, { x: 55, opacity: 0, scale: 0.75 });

gsap.set(boxes, { x: -95, opacity: 0, scale: 0.9 });
      gsap.set(product, { x: 55, opacity: 0, scale: 0.75 });
